regions.label: Source hierarchy and correction rules
regions.goldSection: Page 09 - Source hierarchy and correction handling

leaves.expectation: A signed source correction or signed DCF has the highest field-level priority when it identifies the field, author, date, reason, and final entry.
A correction does not erase the original visible entry.
Checked, unchecked, crossed, and disabled states must remain distinct and bound to their exact labels.
A system snapshot remains evidence of its own timestamp even if a later signed clarification changes the field value.
A laboratory amendment supersedes the analytical value it identifies but not independent custody timestamps.
A signed CAPA due date controls over a working-tracker due date while both remain in the audit trail.

regions.label: Controlled-record repository import audit
regions.goldSection: Page 24 - Controlled-record receipt and import audit

leaves.expectation: The region preserves the source field schema in order: Object, Repository key, Received, Custodian, Integrity check, Import disposition, Reviewer.

The source row order is recoverable as: DCF Q-77 -> Receipt SH-204-45 -> Lab AC-77152 -> ePRO competency -> Dose worksheet -> PI review sheet -> Visit adjudication -> CAPA approval -> Action assignment -> Masking template.
For DCF Q-77, Import disposition is Imported v1.
For Receipt SH-204-45, Repository key is LAB/AC76881/R2.
For Lab AC-77152, Import disposition is Imported amended.
For Visit adjudication, Import disposition is Staged for upload.
For CAPA approval, Integrity check is Dual signature valid.
For Masking template, Integrity check is Unsigned draft.
For Masking template, Import disposition is Quarantined draft.

regions.label: Repository import scope limitation
regions.goldSection: Page 24 - Controlled-record receipt and import audit

leaves.expectation: Imported, staged, and quarantined are distinct repository states.
An import disposition does not decide which value controls a clinical field.
The import audit does not close a query or deviation.
